sugar_baby.name: Sugar Baby - Watermelon
sugar_baby.description: Early maturing watermelon variety with excellent fruit quality and high yield potential
sugar_baby.overview: Sugar Baby is a popular early maturing watermelon variety known for its sweet, crisp flesh and excellent transport quality. This variety is ideal for both commercial and home garden cultivation.
key_features: High yield potential (48-50 tons per acre)
sugar_baby.growing_requirements: Prefers warm climates with well-drained soil. Requires consistent moisture during fruit development.
sugar_baby.planting_instructions: Plant seeds 1-2 cm deep in well-prepared soil. Space plants 100cm x 100cm apart.
sugar_baby.harvesting: Harvest when fruits reach full size and develop proper color. Handle carefully to avoid damage.
sugar_baby.disease_resistance: Good resistance to common watermelon diseases
sugar_baby.yield_potential: 48-50 tons per acre under good management
usage_instructions: Plant in warm season when soil temperature is above 20°C
Provide consistent moisture during fruit development
Apply balanced fertilizer for optimal growth
Harvest at proper maturity for best quality
sugar_baby.application_method: Direct seeding recommended. Plant in rows with proper spacing for optimal growth.

julie_f1.name: Julie F1 - Watermelon
julie_f1.description: High-yielding hybrid watermelon with oblong shaped fruits and excellent disease resistance
julie_f1.overview: Julie F1 is a popular hybrid watermelon variety known for its oblong shaped fruits, very sweet red flesh, and excellent disease resistance.
key_features: Tolerant to fusarium wilt and anthracnose
High yield potential (35-40 tons per acre)
julie_f1.growing_requirements: Thrives in warm climates with well-drained soil. Requires consistent moisture.
julie_f1.planting_instructions: Plant seeds 1-2 cm deep. Space plants 150cm x 100cm apart.
julie_f1.harvesting: Harvest when fruits reach full size and develop proper color.
julie_f1.disease_resistance: Tolerant to fusarium wilt and anthracnose
julie_f1.application_method: Direct seeding recommended with proper spacing.

drumhead_cabbage.name: Drumhead - Cabbage
drumhead_cabbage.description: Cool season cabbage variety with large, round heads and excellent storage quality
drumhead_cabbage.overview: Drumhead cabbage is a cool season variety known for its large, round heads and excellent storage quality. Ideal for areas with moderate to heavy rainfall.
key_features: High yield potential (32 tons per acre)
drumhead_cabbage.growing_requirements: Prefers cool conditions with well-distributed, moderate to heavy rainfall.
drumhead_cabbage.planting_instructions: Transplant seedlings when they have 4-6 true leaves. Space plants 60cm x 45cm apart.
drumhead_cabbage.harvesting: Harvest when heads are firm and reach desired size.
drumhead_cabbage.disease_resistance: Good resistance to common cabbage diseases

copenhagen_cabbage.name: Copenhagen - Cabbage
copenhagen_cabbage.description: Early maturing cabbage variety with excellent quality and high yield potential
copenhagen_cabbage.overview: Copenhagen cabbage is an early maturing variety known for its excellent quality and high yield potential. Suitable for well-drained soils with low organic matter.
key_features: High yield potential (34 tons per acre)
copenhagen_cabbage.growing_requirements: Prefers well-drained soil with low organic matter content.
copenhagen_cabbage.planting_instructions: Transplant seedlings when ready. Space plants 60cm x 45cm apart.
copenhagen_cabbage.harvesting: Harvest when heads are firm and reach desired size.
copenhagen_cabbage.disease_resistance: Good resistance to common cabbage diseases

coriander_dhania.name: Coriander Dhania
coriander_dhania.description: Traditional coriander variety with excellent flavor and aroma, perfect for culinary use
coriander_dhania.overview: Coriander Dhania is a traditional variety known for its excellent flavor and aroma. Perfect for culinary use and easy to grow.
coriander_dhania.growing_requirements: Prefers well-drained soil with good organic matter content.
coriander_dhania.planting_instructions: Plant seeds in drills 30cm apart at 2.5-3.5cm depth.
coriander_dhania.harvesting: Harvest leaves when needed. Seeds can be harvested when mature.
coriander_dhania.disease_resistance: Good resistance to common herb diseases
